tools.name: attributeNamespaces.deleteAttributeNamespace
tools.description:
Delete an attribute namespace and all attributes below.

This operation is the same as the one defined with [DELETE /attribute/{namespace}/_meta](#/Attributes/delete_attribute__namespace___meta).

tools.name: attributeNamespaces.deleteAttributeNamespaceMeta
tools.description:
Delete an attribute namespace and all attributes below.

This operation is the same as the one defined with [DELETE /attribute/{namespace}](#/Attributes/delete_attribute__namespace_).

tools.name: attributeNamespaces.getAttribute
tools.description: List all attribute namespaces.

tools.name: attributeNamespaces.getAttributeNamespace
tools.description: List all attributes under a given attribute namespace.

tools.name: attributeNamespaces.getAttributeNamespaceMeta
tools.description: Shows attribute namespace.

tools.name: attributeNamespaces.postAttributeNamespaceMeta
tools.description:
This endpoint can be used for both, creating an attribute namespace and updating it:
  * If the attribute namespace passed as parameter doesn't exist, it will create the attribute namespace.
  * If the attribute namespace passed as parameter already exists, it will update the namespace attribute.

This operation is the same as the one defined with [PUT](#/Attributes/put_attribute__namespace___meta).

tools.name: attributeNamespaces.putAttributeNamespaceMeta
tools.description:
This endpoint can be used for both, creating an attribute namespace and updating it:
  * If the attribute namespace passed as parameter doesn't exist, it will create the attribute namespace.
  * If the attribute namespace passed as parameter already exists, it will update the namespace attribute.

This operation is the same as the one defined with [POST](#/Attributes/post_attribute__namespace___meta).

tools.name: attributes.deleteAttributeNamespaceAttributeName
tools.description:
Delete an attribute and all its values in projects or packages.

This operation is the same as the one defined with [DELETE /attribute/{namespace}/{attribute_name}/_meta](#/Attributes/delete_attribute__namespace___attribute_name___meta)

tools.name: attributes.deleteAttributeNamespaceAttributeNameMeta
tools.description:
Delete an attribute and all its values in projects or packages.

This operation is the same as the one defined with [DELETE /attribute/{namespace}/{attribute_name}](#/Attributes/delete_attribute__namespace___attribute_name_)

tools.name: attributes.getAttributeNamespaceAttributeNameMeta
tools.description: Shows attribute.

tools.name: attributes.postAttributeNamespaceAttributeNameMeta
tools.description:
This endpoint can be used for both, creating an attribute and updating it:
  * If the attribute passed as parameter doesn't exist, it will create the attribute.
  * If the attribute passed as parameter already exists, it will update the attribute.

This operation is the same as the one defined with [PUT](#/Attributes/put_attribute__namespace___attribute_name___meta).

tools.name: attributes.putAttributeNamespaceAttributeNameMeta
tools.description:
This endpoint can be used for both, creating an attribute and updating it:
  * If the attribute passed as parameter doesn't exist, it will create the attribute.
  * If the attribute passed as parameter already exists, it will update the attribute.

This operation is the same as the one defined with [POST](#/Attributes/post_attribute__namespace___attribute_name___meta).

tools.name: build.getBuild
tools.description: Get a simple directory listing of all projects

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectName
tools.description: Get a simple directory listing of all repositories for the specified project

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectNameRepositoryName
tools.description: List of all architectures the specified project builds against a given repository.

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectNameRepositoryNameArchitectureNameBuilddepinfo
tools.description: Show the build dependencies of packages that are part of the project, for a given repository and architecture.

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectNameRepositoryNameArchitectureNamePackageName
tools.description: List binaries built by the sources of the specified package.

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectNameRepositoryNameArchitectureNamePackageNameJobstatus
tools.description: Show the build status of a currently running build job. Shows an empty result if no build job is running.

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectNameRepositoryNameArchitectureNamePackageNameReason
tools.description: Details about the reason of the lastly triggered build.

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectNameRepositoryNameArchitectureNameRepository
tools.description: List all binaries (produced by all packages of the given project) for the specified repository and architecture.

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectNameRepositoryNameBuildconfig
tools.description: Show the build configuration for the specified repository. Includes all base package requirements, mappings and macros.

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectNameResult
tools.description: Get the build results for packages, architectures and repositories of the specified project.

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectRepositoryArchitecturePackageFile
tools.description: Given a project, repository, architecture and package, retrieve the given file's content.

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectRepositoryArchitecturePackageFileViewFileinfo
tools.description: Given a project, repository, architecture and package, retrieve the given artifact's details.

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectRepositoryArchPackageBuildinfo
tools.description: This endpoint returns information about some specific artifact

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectRepositoryArchPackageHistory
tools.description: This endpoint returns the build history for a specific artifact

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectRepositoryArchPackageLog
tools.description: This endpoint returns the last log file for a specific build artifact

tools.name: build.getBuildProjectRepositoryArchPackageStatus
tools.description: This endpoint returns the building status for a specific artifact

tools.name: build.postBuildProjectName
tools.description: Apply different actions on builds/build processes of the specified project

tools.name: build.putBuildProjectRepositoryArchitecturePackageFile
tools.description:
Given a project, repository, architecture and package, update the given file's content.

This is for Admins only.

tools.name: configuration.getConfiguration
tools.description: Display the configuration of this Open Build Service instance.

tools.name: configuration.putConfiguration
tools.description:
Update the configuration of this Open Build Service instance.

This is for admins only.

tools.name: distributions.deleteDistributionsDistributionId
tools.description:
Delete a distribution.

This is only for admins.

tools.name: distributions.getDistributions
tools.description:
List all distributions that can be build against.
This will not list distributions that are available via [interconnect](https://openbuildservice.org/help/manuals/obs-user-guide/cha.obs.concepts.html#id-1.5.10.3.5).
Check /distributions/include_remotes for this.

tools.name: distributions.getDistributionsDistributionId
tools.description: Show a distribution that can be build against.

tools.name: distributions.getDistributionsIncludeRemotes
tools.description: List all distributions that can be build against, including the ones provided by the interconnect.

tools.name: distributions.postDistributions
tools.description:
Create a distribution.

This is only for admins.

tools.name: distributions.putDistributionsBulkReplace
tools.description:
Bulk replace all distributions.

This is only for admins.

tools.name: distributions.putDistributionsDistributionId
tools.description: Update the data of a distribution.

tools.name: generalInformation.getAbout
tools.description: Get generic information about the API.

tools.name: generalInformation.getArchitectures
tools.description: Get a list of all known architectures known to OBS in general. This is not the list of architectures provided by this instance. Check the schedulers element from the `/configuration` route for this.

tools.name: generalInformation.getArchitecturesArchitectureName
tools.description: Show information about one architecture.

tools.name: groups.deleteGroupGroupTitle
tools.description: Delete a group.

tools.name: groups.getGroup
tools.description: List available groups.

tools.name: groups.getGroupGroupTitle
tools.description: Read group data.

tools.name: groups.postGroupGroupTitle
tools.description: Modify group data.

tools.name: groups.putGroupGroupTitle
tools.description: Write group data.

tools.name: issueTrackers.deleteIssueTrackersIssueTrackerName
tools.description: Delete an issue tracker.

tools.name: issueTrackers.getIssueTrackers
tools.description: Get the list of issue trackers.

tools.name: issueTrackers.getIssueTrackersIssueTrackerName
tools.description: Show an issue tracker.

tools.name: issueTrackers.getIssueTrackersIssueTrackerNameIssuesIssueName
tools.description: Show an issue of an issue tracker.

tools.name: issueTrackers.postIssueTrackers
tools.description: Create an issue tracker.

tools.name: issueTrackers.putIssueTrackersIssueTrackerName
tools.description: Update the issue tracker given as path parameter. If it is not found, create it.

tools.name: person.deletePersonLoginTokenId
tools.description: Delete a token of the specified person.

tools.name: person.getPerson
tools.description: List all people.

tools.name: person.getPersonLogin
tools.description: Get details about a person

tools.name: person.getPersonLoginGroup
tools.description: List the groups the person belongs to.

tools.name: person.getPersonLoginToken
tools.description: List the authentication tokens of a person.

tools.name: person.postPerson
tools.description:
Allows executing command on the person endpoint.

As of now, the only command allowed is 'register'.

tools.name: person.postPersonLogin
tools.description: Perform changes on a registered person

tools.name: person.postPersonLoginToken
tools.description:
Create a new authentication token for a person.

The token may be limited to a specific package. In this case the query parameters
`project` and `package` should be provided.

With an empty request body, a token of the default kind 'runservice' is created.

tools.name: person.postPersonRegister
tools.description: Registers a new person

tools.name: person.putPersonLogin
tools.description: Update person

tools.name: publishedBinaries.getPublished
tools.description: Get a list of all the projects, all of them are considered published.

tools.name: publishedBinaries.getPublishedProjectName
tools.description: Get a list of the repositories of the project that already have published binaries.

tools.name: publishedBinaries.getPublishedProjectNameRepositoryName
tools.description: Get a list of architectures' directories and other files (.repo, .ymp, etc.) present at the level project/repository of the directory tree where the published binaries are stored.

tools.name: publishedBinaries.getPublishedProjectNameRepositoryNameArchitectureName
tools.description: Get a list of binaries and other files present at the level project/repository/architecture of the directory tree as a result of successful building and publishing processes.

tools.name: publishedBinaries.getPublishedProjectNameRepositoryNameArchitectureNameBinaryFilename
tools.description:
Allow to download the binary file that was published and stored under the directory given by project/repository/architecture/.
Can response with Media Types like application/x-rpm, text/xml, etc.
NOTE: Use this only if you absolutely have to, as it doesn't use the redirector.

tools.name: publishedBinaries.getPublishedProjectNameRepositoryNameArchitectureNameBinaryFilenameViewYmp
tools.description:
Generate a ymp pattern, which contains the list of packages needed for intalling certain software without having to create dependencies between them.
Read more about patterns [in this tutorial](https://en.opensuse.org/openSUSE:Build_Service_Tutorial#Create_Patterns).

tools.name: publishedBinaries.getPublishedProjectNameRepositoryNameViewStatus
tools.description: Get information about the build process (build id, start time, etc.) for the pair project and repository.

tools.name: request.deleteRequestId
tools.description: Delete a given request.

tools.name: request.getRequest
tools.description: Get a simple directory listing of all requests

tools.name: request.getRequestId
tools.description: Show details about a specified request.

tools.name: request.getRequestViewCollection
tools.description:
Get a collection of requests for a specified target. **NOTE:**
You need to set at least one of the following parameters in order
to use this endpoint:

* `user`
* `project`
* `package`
* `states`
* `types`
* `ids`

tools.name: request.postRequest
tools.description: Create a request

tools.name: request.postRequestId
tools.description: Apply certain actions on a specified request.

tools.name: request.postRequestIdCmdDiff
tools.description: Get the diff for all packages affected by the request.

tools.name: request.putRequestId
tools.description:
Modify a given request.

**NOTE:** Certain parts of a request can only be changed by admins.

tools.name: workers.getWorkerArchitectureNameWorkerId
tools.description:
Lists capabilites of a worker.

This can be useful when checking for constraints.

This operation is the same as `GET /worker/capability/{architecture_name}:{worker_id}`.

tools.name: workers.getWorkerStatus
tools.description:
Lists status of workers, running jobs, waiting jobs, status of the backend services and general statistics.

Other ways to obtain the same information as with this endpoint are:
  * `GET /worker/_status`
  * `GET /status/workerstatus`
  * `GET /build/_workerstatus`

tools.name: workers.postWorkerCmdCheckconstraints
tools.description:
Given a project, package, repository and architecture, list workers which can build with that restrictions, and also match a constraints filter.

This endpoint doesn't create or modify any data.

More information about constraints can be found in the
[user guide](https://openbuildservice.org/help/manuals/obs-user-guide/cha.obs.build_job_constraints.html).

This operation is the same as `POST /worker/command/run?cmd=checkconstraints`.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
